sections.paragraph:
and דֹּוב masc. epicœne a bear, so called from its slow pace (see the root דָּבַב ), 1Sa 17:34, 36 1Sa 17:36, 37 1Sa 17:37 2Sa 17:8 Pro 17:12 Hos 13:8, דֹּב שַׁבּוּל “a bear (i.e. she bear) robbed of its whelps.” Plur. דֻּבִּים f. she bears, 2Ki 2:24. (Arab. دُبُّ, دُبَّةُ, a he bear, a she bear.)

דב

Ch. id. Dan 7:5.
